content.content:
1→# TestResultsAggregator Module
     2→# Parses JUnit XML and JSON test result files, aggregates across matrix builds,
     3→# detects flaky tests, and generates GitHub Actions-style markdown summaries.
     4→#
     5→# TDD approach: tests in TestResultsAggregator.Tests.ps1 were written FIRST,
     6→# then each function below was implemented to make the corresponding tests pass.
     7→
     8→Set-StrictMode -Latest
     9→$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
    10→
    11→# =============================================================================
    12→# GREEN PHASE 1: Import-JUnitResults
    13→# Parses a JUnit XML file into a standardised result object.
    14→# =============================================================================
    15→function Import-JUnitResults {
    16→    [CmdletBinding()]
    17→    [OutputType([hashtable])]
    18→    param(
    19→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
    20→        [string]$Path
    21→    )
    22→
    23→    # Validate the file exists
    24→    if (-not (Test-Path -Path $Path)) {
    25→        throw "File does not exist: $Path"
    26→    }
    27→
    28→    # Attempt to load and parse the XML
    29→    try {
    30→        [xml]$xml = Get-Content -Path $Path -Raw
    31→    }
    32→    catch {
    33→        throw "Failed to parse JUnit XML file '$Path': $($_.Exception.Message)"
    34→    }
    35→
    36→    # Extract the root <testsuites> element
    37→    $testSuitesNode = $xml.testsuites
    38→    [string]$runName = [string]$testSuitesNode.name
    39→    [double]$totalDuration = [double]$testSuitesNode.time
    40→
    41→    # Build a flat list of test case objects from all <testsuite>/<testcase> elements
    42→    [System.Collections.ArrayList]$testCases = [System.Collections.ArrayList]::new()
    43→
    44→    foreach ($suite in $testSuitesNode.testsuite) {
    45→        foreach ($tc in $suite.testcase) {
    46→            [string]$status = 'passed'
    47→            [string]$failureMessage = ''
    48→
    49→            # Check for <failure> child element
    50→            if ($null -ne $tc.failure) {
    51→                $status = 'failed'
    52→                $failureMessage = [string]$tc.failure.message
    53→            }
    54→            # Check for <skipped> child element
    55→            elseif ($null -ne $tc.skipped) {
    56→                $status = 'skipped'
    57→            }
    58→
    59→            [hashtable]$testCase = @{
    60→                Name           = [string]$tc.name
    61→                ClassName      = [string]$tc.classname
    62→                Status         = [string]$status
    63→                Duration       = [double]$tc.time
    64→                FailureMessage = [string]$failureMessage
    65→            }
    66→            [void]$testCases.Add($testCase)
    67→        }
    68→    }
    69→
    70→    [hashtable]$result = @{
    71→        RunName       = $runName
    72→        TotalDuration = $totalDuration
    73→        TestCases     = [array]$testCases.ToArray()
    74→    }
    75→
    76→    return $result
    77→}
    78→
    79→# =============================================================================
    80→# GREEN PHASE 2: Import-JsonTestResults
    81→# Parses a JSON test result file into the same standardised result object.
    82→# =============================================================================
    83→function Import-JsonTestResults {
    84→    [CmdletBinding()]
    85→    [OutputType([hashtable])]
    86→    param(
    87→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
    88→        [string]$Path
    89→    )
    90→
    91→    # Validate the file exists
    92→    if (-not (Test-Path -Path $Path)) {
    93→        throw "File does not exist: $Path"
    94→    }
    95→
    96→    # Attempt to load and parse the JSON
    97→    try {
    98→        [string]$raw = Get-Content -Path $Path -Raw
    99→        $json = $raw | ConvertFrom-Json
   100→    }
   101→    catch {
   102→        throw "Failed to parse JSON file '$Path': $($_.Exception.Message)"
   103→    }
   104→
   105→    [string]$runName = [string]$json.runName
   106→    [double]$totalDuration = [double]0
   107→
   108→    # Build a flat list of test case objects from all suites
   109→    [System.Collections.ArrayList]$testCases = [System.Collections.ArrayList]::new()
   110→
   111→    foreach ($suite in $json.suites) {
   112→        foreach ($test in $suite.tests) {
   113→            [string]$failureMessage = ''
   114→            if ($test.status -eq 'failed' -and $null -ne $test.error) {
   115→                $failureMessage = [string]$test.error.message
   116→            }
   117→
   118→            [double]$duration = [double]$test.duration
   119→            $totalDuration = $totalDuration + $duration
   120→
   121→            [hashtable]$testCase = @{
   122→                Name           = [string]$test.name
   123→                ClassName      = [string]$test.classname
   124→                Status         = [string]$test.status
   125→                Duration       = $duration
   126→                FailureMessage = $failureMessage
   127→            }
   128→            [void]$testCases.Add($testCase)
   129→        }
   130→    }
   131→
   132→    [hashtable]$result = @{
   133→        RunName       = $runName
   134→        TotalDuration = $totalDuration
   135→        TestCases     = [array]$testCases.ToArray()
   136→    }
   137→
   138→    return $result
   139→}
   140→
   141→# =============================================================================
   142→# GREEN PHASE 3: Merge-TestResults
   143→# Aggregates results from multiple runs into totals and per-test detail maps.
   144→# =============================================================================
   145→function Merge-TestResults {
   146→    [CmdletBinding()]
   147→    [OutputType([hashtable])]
   148→    param(
   149→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
   150→        [hashtable[]]$Results
   151→    )
   152→
   153→    if ($Results.Count -eq 0) {
   154→        throw 'Results must contain at least one test run.'
   155→    }
   156→
   157→    [int]$totalPassed = 0
   158→    [int]$totalFailed = 0
   159→    [int]$totalSkipped = 0
   160→    [double]$totalDuration = [double]0
   161→    [int]$runCount = $Results.Count
   162→
   163→    # Per-test detail map: key = "ClassName.Name", value = outcomes across runs
   164→    [hashtable]$testDetails = @{}
   165→
   166→    foreach ($run in $Results) {
   167→        $totalDuration = $totalDuration + [double]$run.TotalDuration
   168→
   169→        foreach ($tc in $run.TestCases) {
   170→            [string]$status = [string]$tc.Status
   171→
   172→            switch ($status) {
   173→                'passed'  { $totalPassed++  }
   174→                'failed'  { $totalFailed++  }
   175→                'skipped' { $totalSkipped++ }
   176→            }
   177→
   178→            # Build a unique key per test
   179→            [string]$key = "$($tc.ClassName).$($tc.Name)"
   180→
   181→            if (-not $testDetails.ContainsKey($key)) {
   182→                $testDetails[$key] = @{
   183→                    Name      = [string]$tc.Name
   184→                    ClassName = [string]$tc.ClassName
   185→                    Outcomes  = [System.Collections.ArrayList]::new()
   186→                }
   187→            }
   188→
   189→            [hashtable]$outcome = @{
   190→                RunName        = [string]$run.RunName
   191→                Status         = $status
   192→                Duration       = [double]$tc.Duration
   193→                FailureMessage = [string]$tc.FailureMessage
   194→            }
   195→            [void]$testDetails[$key].Outcomes.Add($outcome)
   196→        }
   197→    }
   198→
   199→    [hashtable]$merged = @{
   200→        TotalPassed  = $totalPassed
   201→        TotalFailed  = $totalFailed
   202→        TotalSkipped = $totalSkipped
   203→        TotalDuration = $totalDuration
   204→        RunCount     = $runCount
   205→        TestDetails  = $testDetails
   206→    }
   207→
   208→    return $merged
   209→}
   210→
   211→# =============================================================================
   212→# GREEN PHASE 4: Get-FlakyTests
   213→# Identifies tests that passed in some runs and failed in others.
   214→# A test is flaky if it has at least one 'passed' AND at least one 'failed'.
   215→# =============================================================================
   216→function Get-FlakyTests {
   217→    [CmdletBinding()]
   218→    [OutputType([hashtable[]])]
   219→    param(
   220→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
   221→        [hashtable]$MergedResults
   222→    )
   223→
   224→    [System.Collections.ArrayList]$flakyTests = [System.Collections.ArrayList]::new()
   225→
   226→    foreach ($key in $MergedResults.TestDetails.Keys) {
   227→        $detail = $MergedResults.TestDetails[$key]
   228→        [System.Collections.ArrayList]$outcomes = $detail.Outcomes
   229→
   230→        [bool]$hasPassed = $false
   231→        [bool]$hasFailed = $false
   232→
   233→        foreach ($outcome in $outcomes) {
   234→            if ([string]$outcome.Status -eq 'passed') {
   235→                $hasPassed = $true
   236→            }
   237→            elseif ([string]$outcome.Status -eq 'failed') {
   238→                $hasFailed = $true
   239→            }
   240→        }
   241→
   242→        # Flaky = seen both passed AND failed across runs
   243→        if ($hasPassed -and $hasFailed) {
   244→            [int]$totalRuns = $outcomes.Count
   245→            [int]$failedRuns = @($outcomes | Where-Object { [string]$_.Status -eq 'failed' }).Count
   246→            [double]$failureRate = [double]$failedRuns / [double]$totalRuns
   247→
   248→            [hashtable]$flakyInfo = @{
   249→                Key         = [string]$key
   250→                Name        = [string]$detail.Name
   251→                ClassName   = [string]$detail.ClassName
   252→                FailureRate = $failureRate
   253→                FailedRuns  = $failedRuns
   254→                TotalRuns   = $totalRuns
   255→                Outcomes    = $outcomes
   256→            }
   257→            [void]$flakyTests.Add($flakyInfo)
   258→        }
   259→    }
   260→
   261→    return [array]$flakyTests.ToArray()
   262→}
   263→
   264→# =============================================================================
   265→# GREEN PHASE 5: New-MarkdownSummary
   266→# Generates a GitHub Actions-compatible markdown summary from aggregated results.
   267→# =============================================================================
   268→function New-MarkdownSummary {
   269→    [CmdletBinding()]
   270→    [OutputType([string])]
   271→    param(
   272→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
   273→        [hashtable]$MergedResults,
   274→
   275→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
   276→        [AllowEmptyCollection()]
   277→        [array]$FlakyTests
   278→    )
   279→
   280→    [System.Text.StringBuilder]$sb = [System.Text.StringBuilder]::new()
   281→
   282→    # Title
   283→    [void]$sb.AppendLine('# Test Results Summary')
   284→    [void]$sb.AppendLine()
   285→
   286→    # Overview section
   287→    [int]$totalTests = $MergedResults.TotalPassed + $MergedResults.TotalFailed + $MergedResults.TotalSkipped
   288→    [string]$durationFormatted = '{0:N2}' -f [double]$MergedResults.TotalDuration
   289→
   290→    [void]$sb.AppendLine("**$totalTests** tests across **$($MergedResults.RunCount)** runs")
   291→    [void]$sb.AppendLine()
   292→
   293→    # Summary table
   294→    [void]$sb.AppendLine('| Metric | Count |')
   295→    [void]$sb.AppendLine('| ------ | ----- |')
   296→    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| Passed | $($MergedResults.TotalPassed) |")
   297→    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| Failed | $($MergedResults.TotalFailed) |")
   298→    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| Skipped | $($MergedResults.TotalSkipped) |")
   299→    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| Duration | ${durationFormatted}s |")
   300→    [void]$sb.AppendLine()
   301→
   302→    # Flaky tests section
   303→    [void]$sb.AppendLine('## Flaky Tests')
   304→    [void]$sb.AppendLine()
   305→
   306→    if ($FlakyTests.Count -eq 0) {
   307→        [void]$sb.AppendLine('No flaky tests detected.')
   308→    }
   309→    else {
   310→        [void]$sb.AppendLine("Found **$($FlakyTests.Count)** flaky test(s):")
   311→        [void]$sb.AppendLine()
   312→        [void]$sb.AppendLine('| Test | Class | Failure Rate | Failed / Total |')
   313→        [void]$sb.AppendLine('| ---- | ----- | ------------ | -------------- |')
   314→
   315→        foreach ($flaky in $FlakyTests) {
   316→            [int]$pct = [int]([double]$flaky.FailureRate * 100)
   317→            [void]$sb.AppendLine("| $($flaky.Name) | $($flaky.ClassName) | ${pct}% | $($flaky.FailedRuns) / $($flaky.TotalRuns) |")
   318→        }
   319→    }
   320→
   321→    [void]$sb.AppendLine()
   322→
   323→    # Failed tests detail section
   324→    [bool]$hasFailures = $MergedResults.TotalFailed -gt 0
   325→    if ($hasFailures) {
   326→        [void]$sb.AppendLine('## Failed Tests')
   327→        [void]$sb.AppendLine()
   328→
   329→        foreach ($key in $MergedResults.TestDetails.Keys) {
   330→            $detail = $MergedResults.TestDetails[$key]
   331→            foreach ($outcome in $detail.Outcomes) {
   332→                if ([string]$outcome.Status -eq 'failed') {
   333→                    [void]$sb.AppendLine("- **$($detail.ClassName).$($detail.Name)** (run: $($outcome.RunName))")
   334→                    if (-not [string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace([string]$outcome.FailureMessage)) {
   335→                        [void]$sb.AppendLine("  > $($outcome.FailureMessage)")
   336→                    }
   337→                }
   338→            }
   339→        }
   340→        [void]$sb.AppendLine()
   341→    }
   342→
   343→    return $sb.ToString()
   344→}
   345→
   346→# =============================================================================
   347→# GREEN PHASE 6: Invoke-TestResultsAggregator
   348→# End-to-end orchestrator: scans a directory for test files, parses them,
   349→# merges results, detects flaky tests, and returns a markdown summary.
   350→# =============================================================================
   351→function Invoke-TestResultsAggregator {
   352→    [CmdletBinding()]
   353→    [OutputType([string])]
   354→    param(
   355→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
   356→        [string]$Path
   357→    )
   358→
   359→    if (-not (Test-Path -Path $Path)) {
   360→        throw "Directory does not exist: $Path"
   361→    }
   362→
   363→    # Discover JUnit XML and JSON files
   364→    [array]$xmlFiles = @(Get-ChildItem -Path $Path -Filter '*.xml' -File)
   365→    [array]$jsonFiles = @(Get-ChildItem -Path $Path -Filter '*.json' -File)
   366→
   367→    if (($xmlFiles.Count + $jsonFiles.Count) -eq 0) {
   368→        throw "No test result files found in '$Path'. Expected .xml or .json files."
   369→    }
   370→
   371→    # Parse all files
   372→    [System.Collections.ArrayList]$allResults = [System.Collections.ArrayList]::new()
   373→
   374→    foreach ($xmlFile in $xmlFiles) {
   375→        [hashtable]$parsed = Import-JUnitResults -Path $xmlFile.FullName
   376→        [void]$allResults.Add($parsed)
   377→    }
   378→
   379→    foreach ($jsonFile in $jsonFiles) {
   380→        [hashtable]$parsed = Import-JsonTestResults -Path $jsonFile.FullName
   381→        [void]$allResults.Add($parsed)
   382→    }
   383→
   384→    # Merge, detect flaky, generate summary
   385→    [hashtable]$merged = Merge-TestResults -Results ([hashtable[]]$allResults.ToArray())
   386→    [array]$flakyTests = @(Get-FlakyTests -MergedResults $merged)
   387→    [string]$summary = New-MarkdownSummary -MergedResults $merged -FlakyTests $flakyTests
   388→
   389→    return $summary
   390→}
   391→
   392→# Export all public functions
   393→Export-ModuleMember -Function @(
   394→    'Import-JUnitResults'
   395→    'Import-JsonTestResults'
   396→    'Merge-TestResults'
   397→    'Get-FlakyTests'
   398→    'New-MarkdownSummary'
   399→    'Invoke-TestResultsAggregator'
   400→)
   401→

# Benchmark Instructions v1

**Version**: v1
**Date**: 2026-04-02
**Purpose**: Measure how well Claude Code agents perform scripting tasks across different language constraints.

## Methodology

### Overview

Each scripting task is executed by a Claude Code agent under 4 different language constraints ("modes") and with 2 different models, yielding **8 runs per task**. Agents receive only the prompt — no conversation context, no prior results.

### Models

| Model ID | Short Name | Input $/Mtok | Output $/Mtok | Cache Read $/Mtok | Cache Write $/Mtok |
|----------|------------|-------------|--------------|-------------------|-------------------|
| claude-opus-4-6 | opus | $15.00 | $75.00 | $1.50 | $18.75 |
| claude-sonnet-4-6 | sonnet | $3.00 | $15.00 | $0.30 | $3.75 |

### Language Modes

1. **default** — No language specified. The agent chooses whatever language it considers best.
2. **powershell** — The agent must use PowerShell.
3. **powershell-strict** — The agent must use PowerShell with strict mode enabled (`Set-StrictMode -Latest`, `$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'`), strongly-typed parameters (e.g., `[string]$Name`, `[int]$Count`), and typed function return values (e.g., `[OutputType([int])]`).
4. **csharp-script** — The agent must use C# via .NET 10 file-based apps (top-level statements in `.cs` files, executed with `dotnet run <file>.cs`).

### TDD Requirement

Every prompt instructs the agent to use **red/green TDD**:
1. Write a failing test first.
2. Write the minimum code to make it pass.
3. Refactor.
4. Repeat for each piece of functionality.
5. Create mocks and test fixtures as necessary.

### Tool Installation

Agents are responsible for installing any tools they need (PowerShell, .NET SDK, Pester, test frameworks, etc.). The runner does **not** pre-install anything. Time spent on tool installation is captured as part of the total run duration and is also extracted separately as `tool_install_duration_ms` by analyzing the CLI stream for install-related commands and their durations.

### Execution

- Tasks run **sequentially** (one at a time).
- Each agent runs in an **isolated workspace directory** containing only a copy of this instructions file.
- No conversation context or prior run data is passed to the agent.

---

## Tasks

### Task 01: CSV Report Generator

1→# TestResultsAggregator Module
     2→# Parses JUnit XML and JSON test result files, aggregates across matrix builds,
     3→# detects flaky tests, and generates GitHub Actions-style markdown summaries.
     4→#
     5→# TDD approach: tests in TestResultsAggregator.Tests.ps1 were written FIRST,
     6→# then each function below was implemented to make the corresponding tests pass.
     7→
     8→Set-StrictMode -Latest
     9→$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
    10→
    11→# =============================================================================
    12→# GREEN PHASE 1: Import-JUnitResults
    13→# Parses a JUnit XML file into a standardised result object.
    14→# =============================================================================
    15→function Import-JUnitResults {
    16→    [CmdletBinding()]
    17→    [OutputType([hashtable])]
    18→    param(
    19→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
    20→        [string]$Path
    21→    )
    22→
    23→    # Validate the file exists
    24→    if (-not (Test-Path -Path $Path)) {
    25→        throw "File does not exist: $Path"
    26→    }
    27→
    28→    # Attempt to load and parse the XML
    29→    try {
    30→        [xml]$xml = Get-Content -Path $Path -Raw
    31→    }
    32→    catch {
    33→        throw "Failed to parse JUnit XML file '$Path': $($_.Exception.Message)"
    34→    }
    35→
    36→    # Extract the root <testsuites> element
    37→    $testSuitesNode = $xml.testsuites
    38→    [string]$runName = [string]$testSuitesNode.name
    39→    [double]$totalDuration = [double]$testSuitesNode.time
    40→
    41→    # Build a flat list of test case objects from all <testsuite>/<testcase> elements
    42→    [System.Collections.ArrayList]$testCases = [System.Collections.ArrayList]::new()
    43→
    44→    foreach ($suite in $testSuitesNode.testsuite) {
    45→        foreach ($tc in $suite.testcase) {
    46→            [string]$status = 'passed'
    47→            [string]$failureMessage = ''
    48→
    49→            # Check for <failure> child element
    50→            if ($null -ne $tc.failure) {
    51→                $status = 'failed'
    52→                $failureMessage = [string]$tc.failure.message
    53→            }
    54→            # Check for <skipped> child element
    55→            elseif ($null -ne $tc.skipped) {
    56→                $status = 'skipped'
    57→            }
    58→
    59→            [hashtable]$testCase = @{
    60→                Name           = [string]$tc.name
    61→                ClassName      = [string]$tc.classname
    62→                Status         = [string]$status
    63→                Duration       = [double]$tc.time
    64→                FailureMessage = [string]$failureMessage
    65→            }
    66→            [void]$testCases.Add($testCase)
    67→        }
    68→    }
    69→
    70→    [hashtable]$result = @{
    71→        RunName       = $runName
    72→        TotalDuration = $totalDuration
    73→        TestCases     = [array]$testCases.ToArray()
    74→    }
    75→
    76→    return $result
    77→}
    78→
    79→# =============================================================================
    80→# GREEN PHASE 2: Import-JsonTestResults
    81→# Parses a JSON test result file into the same standardised result object.
    82→# =============================================================================
    83→function Import-JsonTestResults {
    84→    [CmdletBinding()]
    85→    [OutputType([hashtable])]
    86→    param(
    87→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
    88→        [string]$Path
    89→    )
    90→
    91→    # Validate the file exists
    92→    if (-not (Test-Path -Path $Path)) {
    93→        throw "File does not exist: $Path"
    94→    }
    95→
    96→    # Attempt to load and parse the JSON
    97→    try {
    98→        [string]$raw = Get-Content -Path $Path -Raw
    99→        $json = $raw | ConvertFrom-Json
   100→    }
   101→    catch {
   102→        throw "Failed to parse JSON file '$Path': $($_.Exception.Message)"
   103→    }
   104→
   105→    [string]$runName = [string]$json.runName
   106→    [double]$totalDuration = [double]0
   107→
   108→    # Build a flat list of test case objects from all suites
   109→    [System.Collections.ArrayList]$testCases = [System.Collections.ArrayList]::new()
   110→
   111→    foreach ($suite in $json.suites) {
   112→        foreach ($test in $suite.tests) {
   113→            [string]$failureMessage = ''
   114→            if ($test.status -eq 'failed' -and $null -ne $test.error) {
   115→                $failureMessage = [string]$test.error.message
   116→            }
   117→
   118→            [double]$duration = [double]$test.duration
   119→            $totalDuration = $totalDuration + $duration
   120→
   121→            [hashtable]$testCase = @{
   122→                Name           = [string]$test.name
   123→                ClassName      = [string]$test.classname
   124→                Status         = [string]$test.status
   125→                Duration       = $duration
   126→                FailureMessage = $failureMessage
   127→            }
   128→            [void]$testCases.Add($testCase)
   129→        }
   130→    }
   131→
   132→    [hashtable]$result = @{
   133→        RunName       = $runName
   134→        TotalDuration = $totalDuration
   135→        TestCases     = [array]$testCases.ToArray()
   136→    }
   137→
   138→    return $result
   139→}
   140→
   141→# =============================================================================
   142→# GREEN PHASE 3: Merge-TestResults
   143→# Aggregates results from multiple runs into totals and per-test detail maps.
   144→# =============================================================================
   145→function Merge-TestResults {
   146→    [CmdletBinding()]
   147→    [OutputType([hashtable])]
   148→    param(
   149→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
   150→        [hashtable[]]$Results
   151→    )
   152→
   153→    if ($Results.Count -eq 0) {
   154→        throw 'Results must contain at least one test run.'
   155→    }
   156→
   157→    [int]$totalPassed = 0
   158→    [int]$totalFailed = 0
   159→    [int]$totalSkipped = 0
   160→    [double]$totalDuration = [double]0
   161→    [int]$runCount = $Results.Count
   162→
   163→    # Per-test detail map: key = "ClassName.Name", value = outcomes across runs
   164→    [hashtable]$testDetails = @{}
   165→
   166→    foreach ($run in $Results) {
   167→        $totalDuration = $totalDuration + [double]$run.TotalDuration
   168→
   169→        foreach ($tc in $run.TestCases) {
   170→            [string]$status = [string]$tc.Status
   171→
   172→            switch ($status) {
   173→                'passed'  { $totalPassed++  }
   174→                'failed'  { $totalFailed++  }
   175→                'skipped' { $totalSkipped++ }
   176→            }
   177→
   178→            # Build a unique key per test
   179→            [string]$key = "$($tc.ClassName).$($tc.Name)"
   180→
   181→            if (-not $testDetails.ContainsKey($key)) {
   182→                $testDetails[$key] = @{
   183→                    Name      = [string]$tc.Name
   184→                    ClassName = [string]$tc.ClassName
   185→                    Outcomes  = [System.Collections.ArrayList]::new()
   186→                }
   187→            }
   188→
   189→            [hashtable]$outcome = @{
   190→                RunName        = [string]$run.RunName
   191→                Status         = $status
   192→                Duration       = [double]$tc.Duration
   193→                FailureMessage = [string]$tc.FailureMessage
   194→            }
   195→            [void]$testDetails[$key].Outcomes.Add($outcome)
   196→        }
   197→    }
   198→
   199→    [hashtable]$merged = @{
   200→        TotalPassed  = $totalPassed
   201→        TotalFailed  = $totalFailed
   202→        TotalSkipped = $totalSkipped
   203→        TotalDuration = $totalDuration
   204→        RunCount     = $runCount
   205→        TestDetails  = $testDetails
   206→    }
   207→
   208→    return $merged
   209→}
   210→
   211→# =============================================================================
   212→# GREEN PHASE 4: Get-FlakyTests
   213→# Identifies tests that passed in some runs and failed in others.
   214→# A test is flaky if it has at least one 'passed' AND at least one 'failed'.
   215→# =============================================================================
   216→function Get-FlakyTests {
   217→    [CmdletBinding()]
   218→    [OutputType([hashtable[]])]
   219→    param(
   220→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
   221→        [hashtable]$MergedResults
   222→    )
   223→
   224→    [System.Collections.ArrayList]$flakyTests = [System.Collections.ArrayList]::new()
   225→
   226→    foreach ($key in $MergedResults.TestDetails.Keys) {
   227→        $detail = $MergedResults.TestDetails[$key]
   228→        [System.Collections.ArrayList]$outcomes = $detail.Outcomes
   229→
   230→        [bool]$hasPassed = $false
   231→        [bool]$hasFailed = $false
   232→
   233→        foreach ($outcome in $outcomes) {
   234→            if ([string]$outcome.Status -eq 'passed') {
   235→                $hasPassed = $true
   236→            }
   237→            elseif ([string]$outcome.Status -eq 'failed') {
   238→                $hasFailed = $true
   239→            }
   240→        }
   241→
   242→        # Flaky = seen both passed AND failed across runs
   243→        if ($hasPassed -and $hasFailed) {
   244→            [int]$totalRuns = $outcomes.Count
   245→            [int]$failedRuns = @($outcomes | Where-Object { [string]$_.Status -eq 'failed' }).Count
   246→            [double]$failureRate = [double]$failedRuns / [double]$totalRuns
   247→
   248→            [hashtable]$flakyInfo = @{
   249→                Key         = [string]$key
   250→                Name        = [string]$detail.Name
   251→                ClassName   = [string]$detail.ClassName
   252→                FailureRate = $failureRate
   253→                FailedRuns  = $failedRuns
   254→                TotalRuns   = $totalRuns
   255→                Outcomes    = $outcomes
   256→            }
   257→            [void]$flakyTests.Add($flakyInfo)
   258→        }
   259→    }
   260→
   261→    return [array]$flakyTests.ToArray()
   262→}
   263→
   264→# =============================================================================
   265→# GREEN PHASE 5: New-MarkdownSummary
   266→# Generates a GitHub Actions-compatible markdown summary from aggregated results.
   267→# =============================================================================
   268→function New-MarkdownSummary {
   269→    [CmdletBinding()]
   270→    [OutputType([string])]
   271→    param(
   272→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
   273→        [hashtable]$MergedResults,
   274→
   275→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
   276→        [AllowEmptyCollection()]
   277→        [array]$FlakyTests
   278→    )
   279→
   280→    [System.Text.StringBuilder]$sb = [System.Text.StringBuilder]::new()
   281→
   282→    # Title
   283→    [void]$sb.AppendLine('# Test Results Summary')
   284→    [void]$sb.AppendLine()
   285→
   286→    # Overview section
   287→    [int]$totalTests = $MergedResults.TotalPassed + $MergedResults.TotalFailed + $MergedResults.TotalSkipped
   288→    [string]$durationFormatted = '{0:N2}' -f [double]$MergedResults.TotalDuration
   289→
   290→    [void]$sb.AppendLine("**$totalTests** tests across **$($MergedResults.RunCount)** runs")
   291→    [void]$sb.AppendLine()
   292→
   293→    # Summary table
   294→    [void]$sb.AppendLine('| Metric | Count |')
   295→    [void]$sb.AppendLine('| ------ | ----- |')

   351→function Invoke-TestResultsAggregator {

    12→# GREEN PHASE 1: Import-JUnitResults
    13→# Parses a JUnit XML file into a standardised result object.
    14→# =============================================================================
    15→function Import-JUnitResults {
    16→    [CmdletBinding()]
    17→    [OutputType([hashtable])]
    18→    param(
    19→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]

    21→    )

    23→    # Validate the file exists
    24→    if (-not (Test-Path -Path $Path)) {

    27→

    29→    try {
    30→        [xml]$xml = Get-Content -Path $Path -Raw
    31→    }
    32→    catch {

    34→    }
    35→
    36→    # Extract the root <testsuites> element
    37→    $testSuitesNode = $xml.testsuites
    38→    [string]$runName = [string]$testSuitesNode.name
    39→    [double]$totalDuration = [double]$testSuitesNode.time
    40→
    41→    # Build a flat list of test case objects from all <testsuite>/<testcase> elements
    42→    [System.Collections.ArrayList]$testCases = [System.Collections.ArrayList]::new()
    43→
    44→    foreach ($suite in $testSuitesNode.testsuite) {
    45→        foreach ($tc in $suite.testcase) {
    46→            [string]$status = 'passed'
    47→            [string]$failureMessage = ''
    48→
    49→            # Check for <failure> child element
    50→            if ($null -ne $tc.failure) {
    51→                $status = 'failed'
    52→                $failureMessage = [string]$tc.failure.message
    53→            }
    54→            # Check for <skipped> child element
    55→            elseif ($null -ne $tc.skipped) {
    56→                $status = 'skipped'
    57→            }
    58→
    59→            [hashtable]$testCase = @{
    60→                Name           = [string]$tc.name
    61→                ClassName      = [string]$tc.classname
    62→                Status         = [string]$status
    63→                Duration       = [double]$tc.time
    64→                FailureMessage = [string]$failureMessage
    65→            }
    66→            [void]$testCases.Add($testCase)
    67→        }
    68→    }
    69→
    70→    [hashtable]$result = @{
    71→        RunName       = $runName
    72→        TotalDuration = $totalDuration
    73→        TestCases     = [array]$testCases.ToArray()

    76→    return $result
    77→}

    89→    )

    91→    # Validate the file exists

    97→    try {

   142→# GREEN PHASE 3: Merge-TestResults
   143→# Aggregates results from multiple runs into totals and per-test detail maps.
   144→# =============================================================================
   145→function Merge-TestResults {
   146→    [CmdletBinding()]
   147→    [OutputType([hashtable])]
   148→    param(
   149→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
   150→        [AllowEmptyCollection()]

   159→    [int]$totalFailed = 0
   160→    [int]$totalSkipped = 0
   161→    [double]$totalDuration = [double]0
   162→    [int]$runCount = $Results.Count
   163→
   164→    # Per-test detail map: key = "ClassName.Name", value = outcomes across runs
   165→    [hashtable]$testDetails = @{}
   166→
   167→    foreach ($run in $Results) {
   168→        $totalDuration = $totalDuration + [double]$run.TotalDuration
   169→
   170→        foreach ($tc in $run.TestCases) {
   171→            [string]$status = [string]$tc.Status
   172→
   173→            switch ($status) {
   174→                'passed'  { $totalPassed++  }
   175→                'failed'  { $totalFailed++  }
   176→                'skipped' { $totalSkipped++ }
   177→            }
   178→
   179→            # Build a unique key per test
   180→            [string]$key = "$($tc.ClassName).$($tc.Name)"
   181→
   182→            if (-not $testDetails.ContainsKey($key)) {
   183→                $testDetails[$key] = @{
   184→                    Name      = [string]$tc.Name
   185→                    ClassName = [string]$tc.ClassName
   186→                    Outcomes  = [System.Collections.ArrayList]::new()
   187→                }
   188→            }
   189→
   190→            [hashtable]$outcome = @{
   191→                RunName        = [string]$run.RunName
   192→                Status         = $status

   194→                FailureMessage = [string]$tc.FailureMessage
   195→            }
   196→            [void]$testDetails[$key].Outcomes.Add($outcome)
   197→        }
   198→    }
   199→
   200→    [hashtable]$merged = @{
   201→        TotalPassed  = $totalPassed
   202→        TotalFailed  = $totalFailed
   203→        TotalSkipped = $totalSkipped
   204→        TotalDuration = $totalDuration
   205→        RunCount     = $runCount
   206→        TestDetails  = $testDetails
   207→    }
   208→
   209→    return $merged
   210→}

   212→# =============================================================================
   213→# GREEN PHASE 4: Get-FlakyTests
   214→# Identifies tests that passed in some runs and failed in others.
   215→# A test is flaky if it has at least one 'passed' AND at least one 'failed'.
   216→# =============================================================================
   217→function Get-FlakyTests {
   218→    [CmdletBinding()]
   219→    [OutputType([hashtable[]])]
   220→    param(
   221→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]

   225→    [System.Collections.ArrayList]$flakyTests = [System.Collections.ArrayList]::new()

   229→        [System.Collections.ArrayList]$outcomes = $detail.Outcomes
   230→
   231→        [bool]$hasPassed = $false
   232→        [bool]$hasFailed = $false
   233→
   234→        foreach ($outcome in $outcomes) {

   236→                $hasPassed = $true
   237→            }
   238→            elseif ([string]$outcome.Status -eq 'failed') {
   239→                $hasFailed = $true
   240→            }
   241→        }
   242→
   243→        # Flaky = seen both passed AND failed across runs
   244→        if ($hasPassed -and $hasFailed) {
   245→            [int]$totalRuns = $outcomes.Count
   246→            [int]$failedRuns = @($outcomes | Where-Object { [string]$_.Status -eq 'failed' }).Count
   247→            [double]$failureRate = [double]$failedRuns / [double]$totalRuns
   248→
   249→            [hashtable]$flakyInfo = @{
   250→                Key         = [string]$key
   251→                Name        = [string]$detail.Name
   252→                ClassName   = [string]$detail.ClassName
   253→                FailureRate = $failureRate
   254→                FailedRuns  = $failedRuns
   255→                TotalRuns   = $totalRuns
   256→                Outcomes    = $outcomes
   257→            }
   258→            [void]$flakyTests.Add($flakyInfo)

   301→    [void]$sb.AppendLine()

   312→        [void]$sb.AppendLine()

   314→        [void]$sb.AppendLine('| ---- | ----- | ------------ | -------------- |')
   315→
   316→        foreach ($flaky in $FlakyTests) {

   318→            [void]$sb.AppendLine("| $($flaky.Name) | $($flaky.ClassName) | ${pct}% | $($flaky.FailedRuns) / $($flaky.TotalRuns) |")

   320→    }

   322→    [void]$sb.AppendLine()

   324→    # Failed tests detail section

   347→# =============================================================================
   348→# GREEN PHASE 6: Invoke-TestResultsAggregator
   349→# End-to-end orchestrator: scans a directory for test files, parses them,

   351→# =============================================================================
   352→function Invoke-TestResultsAggregator {
   353→    [CmdletBinding()]
   354→    [OutputType([string])]
   355→    param(

pwsh -File run-tests.ps1

pwsh -c 'Invoke-Pester -Path "./TestResultsAggregator.Tests.ps1" -Output Detailed'
